questions.question: Who is the author of the Lithuanian novel “The Last Day”, which was recently translated into Hindi by the Sahitya Akademi?
questions.explanation: Sahitya Akademi released the Hindi translation of the acclaimed Lithuanian novel The Last Day, titled “Antim Din”, in New Delhi. The event was held at Rabindra Bhawan in collaboration with the Embassy of Lithuania. The novel is written by Lithuanian-Ukrainian author Jaroslavas Melnikas. Madhav Kaushik, President of Sahitya Akademi, called it a milestone in promoting world literature in Indian languages. It is the first-ever translation of a Lithuanian fiction book into Hindi. The novel explores themes of existential change and cultural identity, fostering literary and cultural collaboration between India and Lithuania.

questions.question: Who has been named the first recipient of the RK Laxman Award for Excellence?
questions.explanation: Aamir Khan will receive the first-ever RK Laxman Award for Excellence on November 23, 2025, at the MCA Cricket Stadium, Pune. The award, instituted by the RK Laxman family, honours creative brilliance and celebrates RK Laxman’s legacy — the creator of The Common Man. Aamir Khan was chosen for his impactful films like Lagaan, Taare Zameen Par, and Dangal, which address social issues and redefine Indian cinema. RK Laxman, one of India’s greatest cartoonists, is known for his cartoon strip “You Said It”, his work in Malgudi Days, and for receiving Padma Bhushan, Padma Vibhushan, and other honours.

questions.question: Which two Indian farmer-owned cooperatives have been ranked as the world’s top cooperatives in the ICA World Cooperative Monitor 2025?
questions.explanation: Two Indian farmer-owned cooperatives — Amul and IFFCO — have been ranked as the world’s top cooperatives in the ICA World Cooperative Monitor 2025 released in Doha, Qatar. Amul (Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation Ltd) ranked number one, and Indian Farmers Fertiliser Cooperative Limited (IFFCO) ranked number two globally. Rankings were based on GDP per capita performance by the International Cooperative Alliance (ICA) headquartered in Brussels. The ICA, in partnership with EURICSE, analyses the global economic and social impact of cooperatives. The recognition coincides with the conclusion of the UN International Year of Cooperatives (IYC) 2025 in Doha.

questions.question: Who was conferred the 3rd Edition of the Manohar Parrikar Yuva Scientist Award in November 2025?
questions.explanation: In November 2025, Dr. Sai Gautam Gopalakrishnan, Associate Professor of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru, Karnataka, received the 3rd Manohar Parrikar Yuva Scientist Award. He was honored for his exceptional work in computational materials science and advanced modeling of complex alloys. His research contributes to sustainable technologies and industrial innovation. The award will be formally presented on 13th December 2025 at the Manohar Parrikar Vigyan Mahotsav, held at the National Institute of Oceanography (NIO), Goa.

questions.question: Which state has introduced India’s first policy for women-centric ergonomic testing of farm machines?
questions.explanation: The Government of Odisha has introduced India’s first policy for women-centric ergonomic testing of farm machines. The Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) was finalised on November 4, 2025, by an expert committee. It ensures that agricultural tools are designed for women’s body structure, strength, and posture. According to the Economic Survey 2024–25, women’s participation in farming rose from 57% in 2017–18 to 64.4% in 2023–24. A pilot study under the Shree Anna Abhiyan (formerly Odisha Millets Mission) tested tools in five districts with women farmers.

questions.question: Khangchendzonga National Park is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) recently rated Khangchendzonga National Park in Sikkim as “good” in its global review of natural World Heritage sites. It is located in Sikkim. The park is part of the Khangchendzonga Biosphere Reserve (KBR), India’s first “mixed” UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized in 2016 for natural and cultural significance. It has 18 glaciers, the largest being Zemu Glacier, one of Asia’s largest.

questions.question: Koraga tribe is mainly found in which states?
questions.explanation: Recently, researchers from Mangalore University and Yenepoya (Deemed-to-be University) discovered a distinct ancestral source in the Koraga tribe, possibly dating back to the Indus Valley Civilisation. The Koraga are an indigenous tribal community mainly found in Dakshina Kannada and Udupi districts of Karnataka and Kasaragod district of Kerala. They are classified as a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG). As per the 2011 Census, their population is 1,582 with 778 males and 804 females.

questions.question: The National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) is implemented by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

questions.explanation: Recently, the National Social Assistance Programme became a key part of India’s social security system. It was started in 1995 as a fully funded Centrally Sponsored Scheme to support people living below the poverty line. The programme is implemented by the Ministry of Rural Development. It has five components that provide financial and food support.

questions.question: Nugu Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Recently, a farmer was killed in a tiger attack near Nugu Wildlife Sanctuary in Mysuru, Karnataka. Nugu Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Mysuru district, Karnataka. It covers about 30 sq.km and includes the Nugu Reservoir in its northern part. Nugu Dam is built across the Nugu River, a tributary of the Cauvery River.

questions.question: Where was the international conference on the urban cooperative credit sector, ‘Co-Op Kumbh 2025’ held?
questions.explanation: The ‘Co-Op Kumbh 2025’ international conference on the urban cooperative credit sector was held in New Delhi to mark the International Year of Cooperatives. The event launched the ‘Delhi Declaration 2025’ as a roadmap for expanding urban cooperative banks. Sahkar Digi-Pay and Sahkar Digi-Loan apps were introduced to promote digital services among small cooperative banks. A plan was announced to set up one urban cooperative bank in every city with over 2 lakh population within five years.
